title: How to Direct a Movie, With Sam Esmail
format: Sam Esmail joins Sean and Amanda to talk through directing a movie stage by stage, after an opening detour through horror vs. rom-coms and the fall's slate.

segments.heading: Catching up on the year
segments.note: horror vs. rom-coms, the summer drought, working through the lists

films.title: Barbarian
films.note: Amanda's exception - plays the tropes for laughs, closer to Scream than to a straight scare.

films.title: Armageddon Time
films.note: Esmail's one recent theater trip; loved the movie, hated the crowd.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: Held No. 1 from Memorial Day to Labor Day because the studios left the calendar empty behind it.

films.title: Nope
films.note: Sean's late-breaking top-five charge on a second watch.

films.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
films.note: Esmail tried it on his daughter; she lasted ten seconds.

films.title: M3GAN
films.note: The Blumhouse trailer that made Esmail laugh out loud - his potential Barbarian.

films.title: Scream
films.note: The horror Amanda does like.

films.title: Fast Company
films.note: Cronenberg's oddball car-racing film; Esmail's verdict: you don't need to watch it.

films.title: Watcher
films.note: The stalker thriller Esmail wouldn't recommend to Amanda - it would land too well.

films.title: How Green Was My Valley
films.note: The Best Picture winner that beat Kane - Esmail's example of a canon entry worth re-litigating.

films.title: Get to Know Your Rabbit
films.note: De Palma's hard-to-find first studio movie, cited as completist trivia.

films.title: Everyone Says I Love You
films.note: Amanda revisited it for the Julia Roberts Hall of Fame and bounced off the Woody Allen wish-fulfillment.

films.title: The Philadelphia Story
films.note: Sean finally caught it off the AFI list; not a favorite.

films.title: His Girl Friday
films.note: Shared favorite - a Howard Hawks point of connection.

films.title: Bringing Up Baby
films.note: Both prefer it to Philadelphia Story.

films.title: High Society
films.note: Sean's blasphemous take: the soft Philadelphia Story remake he likes better.

segments.heading: How to Direct a Movie
segments.note: Esmail on the five stages, built around Leave the World Behind

films.title: Leave the World Behind
films.note: The new film - his Rumaan Alam adaptation with Julia Roberts, changed heavily from the book. Prep and post are the joys; production is the nightmare.

films.title: Comet
films.note: Esmail's first feature, where he met Emmy Rossum.

films.title: Tár
films.note: The heavily written movie Sean would tighten in a spot or two; near five stars for him.

films.title: Taxi Driver
films.note: The subject of Tarantino's "what if De Palma directed it" chapter - imagining other turns while flagging what Scorsese got wrong.

films.title: The Good Nurse
films.note: Esmail defends the craft - Redmayne, Chastain, Jodie Lee Lipes's photography - against a script the hosts call the problem.

films.title: Ambulance
films.note: Sean's cause: Michael Bay's drone work as a model for where action movies should go; Esmail and Amanda unmoved.

films.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark
films.note: Sean's counter-model - Spielberg always knowing where to put the camera.

films.title: The Gray Man
films.note: Same drone toys as Ambulance, but it showed Sean nothing new.

films.title: Avatar
films.note: Sean's proof that a huge action film needn't rely on fast cutting.

films.title: Avatar: The Way of Water
films.note: The sequel, not yet seen at recording.

films.title: The Hospital
films.note: Paddy Chayefsky reference Sean drops - not the bar he's holding The Good Nurse to.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: Best Picture winner Esmail still can't fully explain the scale of; also his Halloween costume source.

films.title: Devil in a Blue Dress
films.note: Carl Franklin, whom Esmail floats as a Big Picture subject.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: Scouted Sean's old Echo Park house; ended up elsewhere.

films.title: Ticket to Paradise
films.note: Amanda on Julia Roberts's jumpsuits as costume-design intent.

films.title: Mother!
films.note: The kind of allegorical child-in-danger film that doesn't hit Esmail the way a realistic one now does.

films.title: Inglourious Basterds
films.note: The 30-minute bar scene as Esmail's runtime argument: length isn't the problem, quality is.

films.title: The Banshees of Inisherin
films.note: Esmail's AD worked on it; nobody expected how big the small, intimate film would land.

films.title: First Man
films.note: Sean's only knock: too tight; why he's excited for Chazelle to go zanier.

films.title: Babylon
films.note: Sean's most-anticipated - the film he can predict least.

films.title: She Said
films.note: Amanda's most-anticipated; a Carey Mulligan and investigative-journalism draw.

films.title: White Noise
films.note: Already seen; would've made the anticipation list otherwise.

films.title: Titanic
films.note: Amanda's long-movie exception.

films.title: Sunrise: A Song of Two Humans
films.note: Esmail's last-great-thing - a Murnau silent that flew by.

films.title: The Bridge on the River Kwai
films.note: A recent catch-up he loves - one of the great endings.

films.title: The Passion of Joan of Arc
films.note: From his BFI-list run - Dreyer.

films.title: The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari
films.note: Among the silents his film-school sister finds boring; context comes later.

films.title: Battleship Potemkin
films.note: "The one with the staircase."

films.title: Chimes at Midnight
films.note: Welles as Falstaff - a four-hour film Esmail weirdly loved young.

films.title: Tokyo Story
films.note: The Ozu he couldn't get through - a Greenwald favorite.

TV: It's the Great Pumpkin, Charlie Brown
Music: Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band

Sunrise: A Song of Two Humans.title: Sunrise: A Song of Two Humans

The Bridge on the River Kwai.title: The Bridge on the River Kwai

The Passion of Joan of Arc.title: The Passion of Joan of Arc
